tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: With only ten percent left and no competing deadlines, finishing this assignment before 13:30 is the clear priority to secure my grade. I can focus entirely on completing it now without worrying about other tasks.
I finished the work just as the deadline hit, but I submitted it unfinished. This feels like a failure to meet my own high standards for academic performance.

desires: Avoid the feeling of having let my own high academic standards down by submitting incomplete work again.
Prevent a drop in trust with Professor prof_assignment that would make future requests feel like a wasted opportunity.
Stop the rising stress before it physically exhausts me further given my existing sleep debt.

goals.description: Submit the assignment immediately to secure the grade and prevent the self-inflicted shame of missing a deadline due to perfectionism.
goals.rationale: Academic achievement is my core value (0.5); letting this slip because I'm 94% done validates my fear that I can't meet standards, which is far more damaging than an imperfect submission.

agent.last_reasoning: Deadline just passed; submitting now prevents the shame of failure despite stress spiking. I'll finish the remaining 5% to secure the grade, then message teammate_A later to avoid friction.

goals.description: Finalize the assignment and submit by 13:30 to lock in the grade without risking a re-do.

goals.description: Maintain silence on all channels until submission is confirmed, avoiding any need to explain delays or manage expectations.
goals.rationale: My avoidant conflict style means interpersonal friction is costly; isolating myself now prevents the stress of managing others' perceptions while I focus on the task.
